content:
First, place the rectangle so that  
A=(0,0), B=(5,0), D=(0,12), C=(5,12).  

Since the distance from E to the line AB (y=0) is 12, we have |y_E|=12; and the distance to AD (x=0) is 1, so |x_E|=1.  Of the four sign‐choices (±1,±12), the only one making ΔABE acute is  
E=(1, −12).  

Any line through E that meets AB at F=(f,0) and CD at G=(g,12) cuts the rectangle into two regions.  One of them is the trapezoid F–B–C–G whose area must be ½·(5·12)=30.  Its area is  
½·( (5−f)+(5−g) )·12 = 6·(10−f−g ) ,  
so  
6(10−f−g)=30  ⇒  f+g=5.  

On the other hand the line through E of slope m has equation  
y+12 = m(x−1).  
– At y=0 ⇒ 0+12 = m(f−1) ⇒ f=1+12/m.  
– At y=12 ⇒12+12= m(g−1) ⇒ g=1+24/m.  

Hence  
(1+12/m)+(1+24/m)=5  ⇒ 2+36/m=5  ⇒  m=12.  

Thus F=(2,0), G=(3,12), and  
FG² = (3−2)²+(12−0)² =1+144=145.  

Answer: \boxed{145}.
